title: lamium garganicum var. molle
intro: Lamium garganicum var. molle, also known as soft-hairy white deadnettle, is a plant species within the Lamiaceae family and is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Traditionally, Lamium garganicum var. molle has been prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
